walking.reason: Walkability of the main towns or villages.

content.quick_summary: A complete travel guide to Santorini.
pros: World-class luxury hotels with infinity pools
Dramatic caldera views from every cliff village
Unique volcanic beaches (Red Beach, Kamari, Perissa)
Fascinating ancient history at Akrotiri (Minoan Pompeii)
Outstanding Greek cuisine with a volcanic twist
Catamaran sunset cruises around the caldera
Museum of Prehistoric Thera with incredible frescoes
cons: Extremely crowded in July and August
Very high prices (most expensive island in Greece)
Cruise ship crowds overwhelm Oia and Fira midday
Not ideal for swimming beaches (rocky coves, black sand)
Difficult for mobility issues — endless stairs everywhere
Can feel overly commercial and touristy
Accommodation books out months in advance
insider_tips: Hike Fira to Oia early (start at 6:30am) for empty paths and golden light.
Sunset from Pyrgos or the Akrotiri Lighthouse has far fewer crowds than Oia Castle.
Stay in Firostefani for the same caldera views as Oia at half the price.
Visit the Museum of Prehistoric Thera in Fira — the frescoes are world-class and most tourists skip it.
Book caldera-view hotels 5-6 months ahead for July and August.
Rent an ATV or scooter to explore the inland villages — that is where the real charm is.
Eat at tavernas in Pyrgos or Megalochori, not on the caldera strip in Fira.
Visit Akrotiri archaeological site midday when it is too hot for the beach — it is covered and cool.
Take the ferry to Thirassia for a day trip to see Santorini as it was 50 years ago.
September is the best month: warm sea, golden light, relaxed atmosphere.
Overnight in Oia gives you the village to yourself at sunrise and after 8pm.
Walk down to Ammoudi Bay for fresh seafood, but book ahead.
mistakes_to_avoid: Only staying in Oia without exploring the rest of the island.
Eating at restaurants on the main caldera strip in Fira — they target tourists and overcharge.
Visiting Oia between 11am and 5pm in peak season.
Bookings made only 1-2 months ahead for summer — you will get overpriced, low-quality rooms.
Relying on public transport — rent a vehicle to truly explore.
Thinking you can swim from caldera-view hotels — you need to go to the east coast beaches.
Skipping the Museum of Prehistoric Thera.
Paying €40+ for a cocktail at a sunset bar without checking the menu prices first.
Not bringing good walking shoes — Santorini is stairs and cobblestones.
Staying only on the caldera side and missing Pyrgos, Megalochori and the traditional south.
